description: Reproducible showcase entries for AI-CAD/CAE backend capabilities

entries.title: Single-part topology optimization
entries.why_impressive: Derives a topology optimization problem from CAE setup, runs SIMP compliance minimization, and writes back optimized geometry as editable Shape IR — all without external solvers for 2D, with structured-voxel experimental support for 3D.
what_it_demonstrates: 2D SIMP with density field and compliance history
Shape IR writeback as extruded_region, density_voxels, or smooth_mesh_proxy
Deterministic contract with limitations and provenance
honesty_boundary: 2D SIMP is plane-stress; out-of-plane loads dropped with warnings
3D SIMP is experimental/structured-voxel, not production-certified
smooth_mesh_proxy is preview-only, not production CAD
Single material, linear elastic only
No manufacturing constraint modeling
limitations: Coarse grids for speed; fine grids need more memory
3D writeback produces watertight mesh proxies, not analytic B-Rep
talking_points: Show the density field evolution — white=material, black=void
Highlight that writeback preserves design-space frame
Point out limitations block: this is reference, not certified CAE

entries.title: Mesh-to-CAD B-Rep STEP reconstruction
entries.why_impressive: Takes a mesh (e.g. from topology optimization or scanned data), segments it into regions, fits analytic surfaces (plane, cylinder, sphere, cone, torus), generates OCC faces, stitches edges, sews into a closed shell, and exports a valid STEP file — with honest fallback when the shell is not closed.
what_it_demonstrates: STEP export only when solid validates
Honest cleanup of stale artifacts on re-run
exportable_artifacts: geometry/reconstructed.step (only when shell closes and validates)
honesty_boundary: Mesh-derived and lossy; not original design history
Analytic-first: plane, cylinder, sphere, cone, torus dominant
Freeform/NURBS fitting is future work
geometry/reconstructed.step never overwrites source STEP
Partial shells do NOT produce STEP; reason recorded in diagnostics
limitations: Small regions or noisy boundaries may be skipped
Roundtrip verification can fail on complex geometry; STEP kept but marked unverified
Not a replacement for native CAD history
talking_points: Show the region graph: how the mesh is segmented
Show fitted surfaces: this is a real cylinder, that is a real plane
Show the stitching plan: matched edges vs gaps
Emphasize: STEP only appears when shell actually closes — no fake exports
Show roundtrip diagnostics: re-imported and checked

entries.title: Assembly-aware topology optimization
entries.why_impressive: Represents a multi-part assembly with parts, interfaces, and connections; resolves interface geometry; builds a simplified proxy CAE model; derives a topology optimization problem for one selected design part while preserving mounting/load interfaces; runs SIMP; writes back an optimized Shape IR for the selected part only.
what_it_demonstrates: Assembly IR with parts, interfaces, and connections
Preserve masks for mounting/load interfaces during optimization
honesty_boundary: Connections are proxies only: rigid_tie, bonded, bolted_proxy, welded_proxy, contact_proxy, spring_proxy
No real nonlinear contact physics — no friction, no real contact forces
No bolt preload modeled
Optimizes one selected design part only; simultaneous multi-part optimization is future work
production_ready: False, contact_physics_modeled: False, bolt_preload_modeled: False
limitations: Proxy CAE model is simplified; full nonlinear FEA is future work
Assembly meshing and per-part mesh coupling are best-effort
Design recommendations are rule-based, not from real solver feedback
talking_points: Show assembly IR: 3 parts, 2 connections, 4 interfaces
Show interface resolution: real geometry extracted from topology
Show preserve regions: mounting holes and load faces stay intact
Show that frozen parts (wall, load_jig) are untouched
Show design recommendations: actionable but advisory

entries.title: Agent-guided parameter design study
entries.why_impressive: An agent proposes parameter changes to a design; the backend validates each proposal against safety rules (bounds, protected variables), executes valid candidates into isolated derived workspaces, ranks them by objective and constraints using deterministic metrics, and allows explicit acceptance of the best safe candidate — all without ever modifying baseline geometry.
what_it_demonstrates: Design study problem contract with variables, bounds, constraints, objective
Candidate patch validation (bounds, protected variables, assembly scope)
Explicit candidate execution into derived workspace
Static metric injection for deterministic evaluation
honesty_boundary: Static metrics only in demo; real CAE evaluation is future work
No autonomous optimization — candidates explicitly proposed and executed one at a time
No baseline overwrite — accepted candidate is derived artifact only
No production approval claimed
Ranking is advisory; safe_to_accept is conservative
Missing metrics produce needs_more_evaluation, not overconfident acceptance
limitations: Real CAE evaluation would require external solver integration
talking_points: Show validation: protected bolt_dia rejected, out-of-bounds rejected
Show execution: each candidate gets its own isolated workspace
Show ranking: feasible good candidate scores highest, infeasible flagged
Show acceptance: explicit, gated, baseline untouched
Show the accepted Shape IR: derived, traceable, with full provenance
